chief_complaint: 68-year-old male with rapid onset of chest pain and sweating for a day.
history: Ticagrelor is an oral antiplatelet drug. The patient developed severe thrombocytopenia after receiving a loading dose of ticagrelor, despite the fact that no other thrombocytopenia-causing medicines were given to him.
exam: Initial cardiac assessment suggested acute coronary syndrome. No other significant findings.
labs: Normal hemogram initially, followed by severe thrombocytopenia after ticagrelor administration. Platelet count gradually recovered after discontinuing ticagrelor.
